app.title: Screenshot to Code
app.update_instruction_placeholder: Tell the AI what to change...

imageupload.upload_prompt: Drag & drop a screenshot here, or click to upload
imageupload.upload_video: Upload a screen recording (.mp4, .mov) or record your screen to clone a whole app (experimental).

importcode.dialog_description: Make sure that the code you're importing is valid HTML.

onboardingnote.or_use_own_key: or use your own OpenAI API key with GPT4 vision access.
onboardingnote.instructions: Follow these instructions to get yourself a key.
onboardingnote.settings_hint: and paste it in the Settings dialog (gear icon above). Your key is only stored in your browser. Never stored on our servers.

picobadge.open_source_project: An open source project by Pico

settings.image_generation_note: More fun with it but if you want to save money, turn it off.
settings.openai_api_key_note: Only stored in your browser. Never stored on servers. Overrides your .env config.
settings.openai_base_url_note: Replace with a proxy URL if you don't want to use the default.
settings.screenshot_url_note: If you want to use URLs directly instead of taking the screenshot yourself, add a ScreenshotOne API key.
settings.editor_theme: Code Editor Theme - requires page refresh to update

termsofservice.enter_email: Enter your email to get started
termsofservice.consent_text: By providing your email, you consent to receiving occasional product updates, and you accept the
termsofservice.run_locally: Prefer to run it yourself locally? This project is open source.
termsofservice.download_github: Download the code and get started on Github.
termsofservice.logos_info: Designers and engineers from these organizations use Screenshot to Code to build interfaces faster.

urlinput.title: Or screenshot a URL...
urlinput.missing_api_key_error: Please add a ScreenshotOne API key in the Settings dialog. This is optional - you can also drag/drop and upload images directly.
urlinput.screenshot_failed_error: Failed to capture screenshot. Look at the console and your backend logs for more details.
